sourceMovie.title: Joy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Joy works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Moxie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Joy clicked for you, Moxie (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Baby Boom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Joy clicked for you, Baby Boom (1987) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Great Gatsby
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Joy clicked for you, The Great Gatsby (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Joy?
faq.answer: Try Moxie, Baby Boom, The Great Gatsby—each shares DNA with Joy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Joy?
faq.answer: Joy (2015) is best described as Drama, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Joy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
